We are in a remediation session for the finding: accounts_password_pam_maxrepeat
 The goal is to set the pam_pwquality maxrepeat parameter to 1 (to prevent more than 1 consecutive repeating character, i.e., at most one consecutive same character? Actually, note: the rule says: "Modify the maxrepeat setting in /etc/security/pwquality.conf to equal to prevent a run of ( + 1) or more identical characters." So we want to set maxrepeat to 1 to disallow two or more consecutive same characters? Let me clarify:

 The pam_pwquality parameter `maxrepeat` sets the maximum number of allowed consecutive same characters in the new password. If set to 1, then two consecutive same characters are not allowed (so max run of same character is 1). The rule says: "prevent a run of ( + 1) or more identical characters", meaning if we set maxrepeat to 1, then a run of 2 or more is disallowed.

 However, note that the previous attempts tried:
   Attempt 1: set to 3 (which would allow up to 3 consecutive same characters, meaning a run of 4 is disallowed) -> but we want the opposite: we want to disallow runs of 2 or more, so we want maxrepeat=1.
   Attempt 2: tried to change from 3 to 1, but it failed? Actually, the scan output says: "Single-rule scan failed for accounts_password_pam_maxrepeat". So we must have done something wrong.

 Let's look at the file: /etc/security/pwquality.conf

 We are to set the line: maxrepeat = 1

 However, note that the remediation script in the triage context does:
   if grep -sq maxrepeat /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf ; then
      sed -i "/maxrepeat/d" /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf
   fi
   Then it appends: "maxrepeat = $var_password_pam_maxrepeat" to /etc/security/pwquality.conf

 But note: the file we are dealing with in the finding is /etc/security/pwquality.conf (not the .d directory). The triage context mentions a script that also looks in /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/.
